description: Efficient arrays of numeric values. Defines an object type which can compactly represent an array of basic values: characters, integers, floating point numbers.

classes.name: array
classes.description: A new array whose items are restricted by typecode, and initialized from the optional initializer value, which must be a list, string, or iterable over elements of the appropriate type. Arrays represent basic values and behave very much like lists, except the type of objects stored in them is constrained.

constructors.name: array

params.name: typecode
params.description: A single character defining the type of values in the array. Valid typecodes: 'c' (char, 1 byte), 'b' (signed char, 1 byte), 'B' (unsigned char, 1 byte), 'u' (Py_UNICODE, 2 bytes), 'h' (signed short, 2 bytes), 'H' (unsigned short, 2 bytes), 'i' (signed int, 2 bytes), 'I' (unsigned int, 2 bytes), 'l' (signed long, 4 bytes), 'L' (unsigned long, 4 bytes), 'f' (float, 4 bytes), 'd' (double, 8 bytes).

params.name: initializer
params.description: An optional initializer value which must be a list, string, or iterable over elements of the appropriate type.

constructors.description: Create a new array with the given typecode and optional initializer.

methods.name: append

params.name: x
params.description: The item to append. Must be of the correct type for the array's typecode.

methods.description: Append a new item with value x to the end of the array.

methods.name: buffer_info

returns.description: A tuple (address, length) giving the current memory address and the length in elements of the buffer used to hold array's contents.

methods.description: Return a tuple (address, length) giving the current memory address and the length in elements of the buffer used to hold array's contents.

methods.name: byteswap
methods.description: Byteswap all items of the array. This is only supported for values which are 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes in size; for other types of values, RuntimeError is raised.

methods.name: count

returns.description: The number of occurrences of x in the array.

methods.description: Return the number of occurrences of x in the array.

methods.name: extend

params.name: iterable
params.description: An iterable of items to append. Can be another array or any iterable.

methods.description: Append items from iterable to the end of the array. If iterable is another array, it must have exactly the same typecode; if not, TypeError will be raised.

methods.name: fromfile

params.name: f
params.description: An open file object to read from.

params.name: n
params.description: The number of items to read.

methods.description: Read n items (as machine values) from the file object f and append them to the end of the array. If less than n items are available, EOFError is raised, but the items that were available are still inserted into the array.

methods.name: fromlist

params.name: list
params.description: A list of items to append.

methods.description: Append items from the list. This is equivalent to for x in list: a.append(x) except that if there is a type error, the array is unchanged.

methods.name: fromstring

params.name: s
params.description: A string of machine values to append.

methods.description: Appends items from the string, interpreting the string as an array of machine values (as if it had been read from a file using the fromfile() method).

methods.name: fromunicode

params.name: s
params.description: A unicode string to extend the array with.

methods.description: Extends this array with data from the given unicode string. The array must be a type 'u' array; otherwise a ValueError is raised.

methods.name: index

returns.description: The smallest index i such that the item at position i is equal to x.

methods.description: Return the smallest i such that i is the index of the first occurrence of x in the array. Raises ValueError if x is not found.

methods.name: insert

params.name: i
params.description: The index before which to insert.

methods.description: Insert a new item with value x in the array before position i. Negative values are treated as being relative to the end of the array.

methods.name: pop

params.name: i
params.description: The index of the item to remove and return. Defaults to -1 (last item).

returns.description: The item removed from the array.

methods.description: Removes the item with the index i from the array and returns it. The optional argument defaults to -1, so that by default the last item is removed and returned.

methods.name: read

params.name: f
params.description: An open file object to read from.

params.name: n
params.description: The number of items to read.

methods.description: Read n items (as machine values) from the file object f and append them to the end of the array. Deprecated since version 1.5.1; use fromfile() instead.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Remove the first occurrence of x from the array. Raises ValueError if x is not found.

methods.name: reverse
methods.description: Reverse the order of the items in the array.

methods.name: tofile

params.name: f
params.description: An open file object to write to.

methods.description: Write all items (as machine values) to the file object f.

methods.name: tolist

returns.description: A list with the same items as the array.

methods.description: Convert the array to an ordinary list with the same items.

methods.name: tostring

returns.description: A string of the machine values in the array (the same sequence of bytes that would be written to a file by tofile()).

methods.description: Convert the array to an array of machine values and return the string representation (the same sequence of bytes that would be written to a file by the tofile() method).

methods.name: tounicode

returns.description: A unicode string of the array contents.

methods.description: Convert the array to a unicode string. The array must be a type 'u' array; otherwise a ValueError is raised.

methods.name: write

params.name: f
params.description: An open file object to write to.

methods.description: Write all items (as machine values) to the file object f. Deprecated since version 1.5.1; use tofile() instead.

fields.name: typecode
fields.description: The typecode character used to create the array.

fields.name: itemsize
fields.description: The length in bytes of one array item in the internal representation.

constants.name: ArrayType
constants.description: Obsolete alias for the array type. Use array.array instead.
